title: PathAwareCritique
description: Set 066 — the saved multi-provider path-aware critique artifact for one session set. Written as path-aware-critique.json beside spec.md at the session-set root. Records the operator-run, multi-provider path-aware review (today GitHub Copilot driving GPT-5.4 + Gemini-Pro over the repo; the first-party tool-loop adapter is deferred to Set 067). The Set-066 S2 close-out gate confirms a valid, content-non-trivial artifact of this shape exists before a set-terminal close when the recorded pathAwareCritique policy is 'required'. This JSON Schema is the STRUCTURAL contract; the runtime gate uses the pure-Python validator ai_router.path_aware_critique.validate_path_aware_critique_artifact, which additionally enforces the multi-provider semantic rule (at least two DISTINCT providers — a constraint JSON Schema cannot express). A structurally-valid artifact with two entries from the same provider passes this schema but is rejected by the Python validator. See docs/path-aware-critique-schema.md.

schemaVersion.description: Envelope schema version. 1 = the v1 contract locked in Set 066 S1.

sessionSetName.description: The slug of the session set this critique covers (the session-set directory basename).

pathAwareCritique.description: The policy level this artifact was produced under. An artifact is only produced for 'advisory' or 'required' sets; 'none' is accepted so the enum stays a single source of truth with the per-set attribute, but a 'none' set produces no artifact in practice.

critiquedAt.description: Optional ISO-8601 timestamp recording when the critique was run.

blastRadius.description: Optional echo of the ai_router.blast_radius recommendation that motivated the policy (pSet / recommended / categories). Advisory metadata; no runtime gate reads it.

critiques.description: The per-provider critique entries. At least two (multi-provider). The runtime Python validator additionally requires at least two DISTINCT providers.

Critique.description: One provider's path-aware critique. provider/model/verdict are required and non-empty; each entry must be content-non-trivial (a non-empty summary OR at least one finding). Extra provider-emitted keys are tolerated (additionalProperties open).

provider.description: The provider that produced this critique (e.g. 'openai', 'google', 'anthropic'). Distinct providers across entries is what makes the artifact multi-provider.

summary.description: The critic's prose verdict / reasoning. Required (non-empty) unless the entry carries at least one finding instead.

findings.description: The critic's structured findings for this artifact. Each finding requires a non-empty description.

Finding.description: One critique finding. Only description is reliably required; severity and category are loose optional strings; extra critic-emitted keys are tolerated. Set 069 S1 adds the optional execution-evidence field evidenceTier (the ORCHESTRATOR-applied tier, never the agent's self-report; an absent tier means ASSERTED). A REPRODUCED finding MUST carry a transcript whose full shape is validated (the if/then below adds the requirement AND the EvidenceTranscript $ref); a transcript on a non-REPRODUCED finding is untyped, ignored supporting context (matching the Python validator). The one rule JSON Schema cannot express is the cross-field replay-hash equality (replay.outputHash == outputHash), enforced by ai_router.evidence_protocol.validate_transcript.

evidenceTier.description: The execution-evidence tier (Set 069). REPRODUCED = reproduced by running a probe, backed by a transcript with a pristine replay; ASSERTED = a read-only claim (the default when absent); HYPOTHESIS = a flagged suspicion. Applied by the orchestrator, never the agent.

EvidenceTranscript.description: A servant-captured execution transcript backing a REPRODUCED finding (Set 069 S1). The probe is a TRUSTED operator-authored command/template (commandId XOR templateId, enforced by oneOf below), never model-authored argv; it ran on a pristine checkout, drives a real public entrypoint (meta-oracle, via EvidenceEntrypoint.kind enum), and was replayed on a second pristine checkout to the same outputHash (a re-runnable falsifier). This $ref applies ONLY on a REPRODUCED finding. The single rule JSON Schema cannot express is the cross-field replay-hash equality (replay.outputHash == outputHash); ai_router.evidence_protocol.validate_transcript adds exactly that.

pinnedRef.description: The git ref / commit the probe ran against.

commandId.description: The operator-authored command id triggered (mutually exclusive with templateId, via the oneOf above).

templateId.description: The operator-authored probe-template id invoked (mutually exclusive with commandId, via the oneOf above).

args.description: The typed, validated args passed to the command/template (optional).

pristineCheckout.description: Must be true - the probe ran on a fresh checkout (this transcript shape applies only to a REPRODUCED finding).

exitCode.description: The probe's raw exit code, or null when killed / timed out.

rawOutput.description: The raw captured stdout+stderr, never summarized (may be elided to a raw head slice).

outputHash.description: sha256:<hex> digest of the raw output, the unit the pristine replay must match.

EvidenceEntrypoint.description: The real PUBLIC entrypoint the probe drove (meta-oracle rule). agent_harness is deliberately NOT an allowed kind here.

EvidenceReplay.description: The replay run on a SECOND pristine checkout. Its outputHash must equal the transcript's outputHash (the one Python-only equality rule).
